description:
## Goal
Consume the T1 outcome and rewrite the IR: when a synthesized pattern-bind fail block is marked exhaustive, delete it and rewire so the bind lowers to pure projection — no `Halt(match_error)`, no dead bb. Refutable binds are untouched.

## Depends on
- T1 (outcome variant + producer must exist).

## Approach
A reducer pass (alongside the existing reducer rewrites) reads the outcome, walks the synthesized fail block, and rewires control flow so the success path is reached directly. Pattern projection (tuple/list-cons element extraction) survives; the fail edge does not.

## Acceptance probe
`{a, b} = pair()` against a known 2-tuple should produce CLIF with no `match_error` halt, no dead block. `[h | t] = xs` where `xs : list(none)` would be flagged earlier as user error; where `xs : nonempty_list(...)`, the fail block is elided; where `xs : list(...)` (may be empty), the fail block stays and runtime `match_error` is preserved.

## BEAM-conform invariant to preserve
Only elide when T1's outcome is published. Refutable destructures keep their runtime check.

## Done when
- New reducer pass exists and is wired into the pipeline.
- For irrefutable destructures: CLIF / interp / AOT all show projection-only lowering, no synthesized fail block.
- For refutable destructures: runtime `match_error` still raised on mismatch.
- `expected.clif` / `expected.outcomes` snapshots updated where they newly reflect the cleanup.
- All tests pass.
title: T2: reducer elides synthesized pattern-bind fail blocks
